name: Sai Wo Duck B1
recipeIngredient: 2 Pieces dried mandarin orange (peeled, size of a half-dollar), presoaked
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the garnish: Loose lettuce leaves.
2. Rub soy sauce over the duckling.
3. Heat enough oil in a large deep-fry pan to cover the bird.
4. Carefully place the duck in the hot oil and fry until the entire bird is well-browned. Remove from oil and drain on absorbent towels.
5. Using a sharp cleaver, slash the duck from breast to lower belly without cutting through the bone.
6. Place the duck back in the pan. Stuff the cavity with chopped celery, green onion, ginger root, black mushrooms, dried mandarin orange pieces, bamboo shoots, star anise seeds, and 1 tsp salt, sugar, pepper, and MSG (if using).
7. Cover and steam the duck for 2-½ hours, replenishing water as needed.
8. Once steamed, remove the platter and set aside to cool.
9. Discard all ingredients except the bamboo shoots and mushrooms; save their juices for gravy.
10. Carefully separate the wings and legs from the carcass using your hands, being careful not to make any additional holes in the skin or disrupt its shape.
11. Spread the duck meat side up on a deep platter.
12. De-bone the legs and place them with the wings.
13. Place the mushrooms and bamboo shoots over the duck meat.
14. Return the duck to the steaming utensil and steam for an additional 20 minutes.
15. Remove the duck from the steamer and carefully turn it upside down on a bed of lettuce leaves, skin side up.
16. Pour the cooking juices into a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
17. Add cornstarch, soy sauce, and salt; stir continuously until the gravy thickens.
18. Pour the thickened gravy over the duck.
19. Garnish with Chinese parsley and serve immediately.
Tools:
Chopsticks
Temperature: HOT
Effort: DIFFICULT
Time: 4 hours
